natural_occurrence.description: Natural orange-red carotenoid pigments extracted from paprika fruits. Capsanthin and capsorubin are the main coloring compounds, responsible for the vibrant red-orange hue of paprika.

manufacturing.description: Extracted from the fruits of sweet red peppers (Capsicum annuum) using solvent extraction with vegetable oil or other approved solvents. The extract is then refined to concentrate the carotenoid content.

other_uses.cosmetics: Used as a colorant in cosmetics and personal care products.
other_uses.industrial: Used in textile dyeing and natural colorants.
other_uses.pharmaceutical: Used as a colorant in pharmaceutical preparations.
other_uses.household: Commonly used as a culinary spice (paprika powder)

eu.conditions: Widely permitted in various food categories

usa.notes: Widely used in cheese, sausages, and snack foods

japan.notes: Also listed as トウガラシ色素 (capsicum pigment)

safety_evaluations.jecfa_adi: 0–24 mg/kg bw/day (for full extract); 0–1.7 mg/kg bw/day (for total carotenoids)
safety_evaluations.efsa_adi: 0.6 mg/kg bw/day (for total carotenoids from E160c)

commonly_found_in: Spanish chorizo and other cured sausages
fun_facts: Capsanthin has potent antioxidant properties and helps protect cells from oxidative damage.
Capsorubin can inhibit UVB-induced DNA damage in skin cells, making it valuable in cosmetic formulations.
Paprika extract has been used as a natural food colorant for centuries in traditional cuisine.
The color intensity of paprika extract depends on the variety of peppers used and their ripeness at harvest.
health_benefits_disclaimer: While capsanthin and capsorubin possess antioxidant properties in laboratory studies, paprika extract is used in foods primarily as a colorant in very small quantities. Any potential health benefits should not be considered medical claims. Consult healthcare professionals for health advice.
